column1: # Reviewed ingredient synonyms.
# A row here lets an allergy BLOCK a medicine. Nothing else does — an unreviewed
# guess produces `warning` at most (spec §2-12). Every row therefore needs a
# reviewer and a source; "it looked similar" is not a source.
# Format (tab-separated):
#   alias <TAB> canonical <TAB> reviewer <TAB> source
# Matching happens on the normalised form: lower case, no dose, no parentheses,
# and with physical-form qualifiers already stripped (granules, micronized,
# anhydrous, …) — see IngredientNormalizer.FORM_QUALIFIERS. So you do NOT need a
# row for "Acetaminophen Granules"; you DO need one for a different chemical name
# or a salt that shares the allergen.
# The reviewer column is READ (IngredientNormalizer.isSigned). `TODO` or a blank
# is not a name, and every unsigned row is listed in a WARN on every boot.
# An unsigned row still blocks, on purpose. Refusing to load it is the DANGEROUS
# direction: drop `paracetamol` and a paracetamol allergy stops matching
# Acetaminophen; drop `dexibuprofen` and word-boundary matching cannot see
# "ibuprofen" inside it. These rows protect people. The risk in an unsigned
# dictionary is the row that ISN'T HERE. See spec §7-1 AR-02.
# TODO(BE-1 + QA, DEV-305): review and extend before the demo. The list below is
# a seed covering the ingredients our personas actually mention. Do not add a row
# without checking it against 식약처 허가정보 `MAIN_INGR_ENG` / `ITEM_INGR_NAME` values.
paracetamol
column4: INN name; USAN and 허가정보 both use "Acetaminophen".

column1: apap
ibuprofen lysine
column4: Salt form; same allergen. 허가정보 lists both.

column1: ibuprofen arginine
dexibuprofen
column4: S-enantiomer of ibuprofen — the same molecule, not a related drug. Basis is chemical identity, NOT drug-class cross-reactivity (we do no class reasoning: spec §7-1 AR-01). 허가정보 lists it separately, and word-boundary matching cannot see "ibuprofen" inside "dexibuprofen", so without this row the product is never blocked.

column1: asa
column4: Standard abbreviation. 허가정보 canonical search term is "Aspirin"; "Acetylsalicylic Acid" returns no results.

column1: aspirin
column4: 허가정보 canonical search term; "Aspirin" returns results while "Acetylsalicylic Acid" does not.
